data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Netflix utilizes a highly scalable microservices architecture that allows independent deployment and scaling of services.
data.technical_spec: Each service runs in its own container, communicating over REST and asynchronous messaging.

data.title: Cloud-Based Infrastructure
data.description: Netflix operates on a cloud infrastructure primarily using AWS, which provides flexibility and dynamism.
data.technical_spec: Use of AWS EC2, S3, and RDS for scalability and storage solutions.

data.title: Chaos Engineering
data.description: Netflix employs Chaos Engineering to ensure system resilience by intentionally introducing failures.
data.technical_spec: Tools like Chaos Monkey automate the simulation of outages and failures.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: A global CDN infrastructure delivers content reliably and quickly to users worldwide.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes Open Connect Appliances for caching content nearer to users.

data.title: Automated Infrastructure
data.description: Automation is extensively used for deploying services and managing operations in their infrastructure.
data.technical_spec: Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment pipelines using Jenkins and Spinnaker.
